extra.reasoning:
Property: rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b must never be jointly true.

rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires exactly when the full 8-way conjunction C = (qt_prolongation and g6pd_deficiency and peptic_ulcer and ckd_dialysis and low_ejection_fraction and polypharmacy and heart_failure and seizure_history) holds. Otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false.

rec_drug_b is affected by two rules: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF qt_prolongation THEN true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF C THEN false).

Consider any input where rec_drug_a is true. Then C holds. Since C holds, qt_prolongation is true, so rec_b_from_trigger matches (wants true, priority 50). But guard_suppress_b also matches (C holds, wants false, priority 90). Per per-output highest-priority-wins conflict resolution, guard_suppress_b (90) beats rec_b_from_trigger (50), so rec_drug_b := false.

Thus whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is forced false by the higher-priority guard whose trigger condition is identical to rec_drug_a's trigger condition. The two outputs can never both be true. (Conversely, when rec_drug_b is true via qt_prolongation without full C, rec_drug_a defaults false.) The property holds over the entire input space.
